title: Crispy Oaxacan Salmon Tacos
description: A quick, crispy Oaxacan‑inspired salmon taco that pairs smoky, spice‑laden fillets with fresh cabbage slaw, creamy avocado, and a tangy yogurt drizzle.

ingredients.name: Oaxacan spice blend (cumin, coriander, smoked paprika, garlic powder, salt, pepper)

instructions: Pat dry the salmon fillets with paper towels and sprinkle both sides with the Oaxacan spice blend.
Zest the lime over the fillets and pour lime juice inside the cavity of the fillets, then let them rest on a plate for 5 minutes to absorb the flavors.

Heat a large skillet over medium‑high heat and drizzle 1 tablespoon of olive oil. When the oil shimmers, add the salmon fillets skin side down and sear for 3–4 minutes until the skin is crisp and a dark golden crust forms.
Flip the fillets and sear the other side for an additional 3 minutes until the salmon is cooked through but still moist in the center.
Transfer the salmon to a cutting board and let it rest for a minute before slicing each fillet into 3–4 bite‑size strips.
Warm the corn tortillas in a dry skillet for 30 seconds on each side or wrap them in foil and heat in a 350°F oven for 5 minutes.
In a small bowl whisk the Greek yogurt with 1 teaspoon honey and a pinch of salt to create a creamy, tangy sauce.
To assemble, place a spoonful of cabbage slaw on each tortilla, top with salmon strips, drizzle with the yogurt sauce, add avocado slices, and finish with a handful of pickled red onions.
Serve the tacos immediately with lime wedges on the side for squeezing.
Enjoy a burst of spicy, citrusy flavors in every bite!
